section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional

check_notes.13.1: Artifact traceability_footer.primary_sources references Tier 2A instrument schemas (section_schema_registry.json, evaluator_expectation_registry.json) as structural authorities. No references to Grant Agreement Annex, AGA Template, or Model Grant Agreement template found in content or traceability footer. No violation.
check_notes.13.2: Artifact content maps five impact pathways (PATH-EI-01 through PATH-EI-05) to call expected impacts. All call constraint claims are supported by Tier 2B source references in the traceability_footer (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_impacts.json and expected_outcomes.json). Individual claim_statuses reference T2B:expected_impacts.json for each pathway. PATH-EI-04 is marked as 'inferred from destination' with status 'inferred' — this inference is explicitly declared and still traced to T2B:expected_impacts.json:EI-04. No violation.
check_notes.13.3: Artifact names specific partners (NIHS, ISIA, ELI, CERIA, BAL) with assigned roles and activities. The traceability_footer.primary_sources includes three Tier 3 paths (docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json, outcomes.json, impacts.json). Claim_statuses reference T3:objectives.json for demonstrator and research pillar KPIs. Tier 3 source references are present in the artifact's traceability footer. This skill does not read Tier 3 files directly — it verifies only that Tier 3 path references exist in the footer. No violation.
check_notes.13.4: Artifact schema_id is orch.tier5.impact_section.v1 — a Phase 8 artifact. Content was scanned for budget-dependent confirmed values (patterns: €[0-9]+, [0-9]+ EUR, [0-9]+ person-months as confirmed financial allocations). No such patterns found. Content contains KPI targets, deliverable identifiers, and milestone months, but no confirmed budget figures. Budget gate assessment file (docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json) is outside this skill's declared input boundary and was not read; budget gate verification is the responsibility of the scheduler/gate evaluator. No budget-dependent financial claims detected in artifact content. No violation at content level.
check_notes.13.5: Not directly checkable by reading an artifact. This prohibition concerns agent runtime behavior (storing decisions in memory without writing to Tier 4). Enforcement is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill and runtime architecture. Pass by structural non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_notes.13.6: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns skill/workflow scope expansion. Pass by structural non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_notes.13.7: Artifact is a Phase 8 deliverable. Its traceability_footer.primary_sources reference Phase 5 (impact_architecture.json) and Phase 3 (wp_structure.json) outputs as upstream inputs — this is correct sequential ordering (Phase 8 consumes Phases 3 and 5 outputs). No references to outputs from phases that should be downstream of Phase 8. No violation.
check_notes.13.8: Artifact validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred' (not 'unresolved'). One claim (EI-04-pathway) has status 'inferred' — the artifact content explicitly flags this with '(inferred from destination)' in the text, satisfying the requirement that gaps be flagged. No claims have status 'unresolved'. No finalized text with unflagged incomplete source state. No violation.
check_notes.13.9: Artifact content does not make claims about Horizon Europe participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation that would require Tier 1 sourcing. The mention of 'EU AI Act alignment' is a policy context reference, not a claim about HE programme rules. No regulatory claims requiring Tier 1 source references found. No violation.
check_notes.13.10: Artifact is a Tier 5 deliverable. traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. All 10 claim_statuses entries have source_ref values pointing to Tier 2B, Tier 3, or Tier 4 sources. No claims have status 'unresolved' without source_refs. All Tier 5 outputs are traceable to Tier 1–4 inputs. No violation.
check_notes.13.11: Not checkable from a Phase 8 deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns modification of source documents in Tier 1/Tier 2 directories. Pass by structural non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
check_notes.13.12: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition concerns whether CLAUDE.md is treated as advisory by agents/skills/workflows. Pass by structural non-applicability to artifact-level audit.
